reviews.review_msg: There was no lecture. Only readings. The "lecture" was spent going over past assignments and group presentations. She rambles and gets lost in her own work. The assigs were full of mistakes even though they were copied and she often didn't know what was on them.Grading criteria was often ignored or incorrect. Impossible to learn.

There are a LOT of weekly readings. The weekly "pre-quizzes" aren't too bad since you get 2+ attempts. Every bi-weekly assignment must be done in groups, so it's difficult to coordinate. Weekly Zoom lectures barely cover the material since group presentations take up most of the time. She's nice during office hours and answers questions in depth.
Easiest 2 classes I have done, went in one single time to class to do a presentation.
Very fun course all in all. Made me rethink how I look at the world and the role linguistics plays in every aspect.

She's nice. But niceness has nothing to with teaching ability. Her class makes no sense at all.. Sorry, the rating I gave is inaccurate, the minimum I could put is a 1. I would've given a 0 because there's no difference in taking her class or not. Didn't learn a single thing. Avoid at all costs.

Taught the class for the first time and used that as an excuse to be totally disorganized. Grading between her and the TA was inconsistent. Averages for the drafts were in the 50%s. Her expectations are nowhere to be found as the whole class never knew what they were doing. Also never answers emails so you won't get any help from her.

Boring, disorganized and rambles. Gives no clear expectations and her feedback is unconstructive. Her first time teaching the class is not an excuse for not creating material that actually work towards creating a good argumentative linguistic paper. She finally gave a guideline about what she wants in papers in the 2nd to last class. Way too late

Prof Nancy was very very helpful and she's very knowledgeable! Even though her lecture was alil boring bcuz of her 'monotone' voice. I'd suggest you to go to her office hours. She provided clearer instruction and in-depth materials during office hours, compared to lecturer. Some of her midterms were quite hard but some were not! and study the text!

She's really nice & kind. However, a bit boring. She reads off the slides which are directly off the textbook. Sometimes it gets confusing. The course w/ her isn't hard. I'm grabbing A's by just reading & taking my own notes from the textbook. If youre willing to teach yourself & have easy tests this is the course for you. I'm taking 481 w/ her too

Nancy is the sweetest lady you will ever meet! However, she uses VERY technical language and abstract definitions of concepts and has trouble coming up with additional examples. Exams were easy though, just the assignments with a few words changed.
